scenarios.name: feature-pr-with-technical-choice
scenarios.prompt: Write a GitHub pull request description for this work. I added a SQL file upload modal to the Migration Application details page. It has two steps: a Files step (drag-and-drop selection with validation) and a Databases step (assigning a target database version per file). On confirm, files upload via XHR with a concurrency limit. I used XHR instead of fetch because the Fetch API can't report upload progress and XHR's upload.onprogress is the only way to get byte-level progress in the browser. The Jira ticket is MIG-7928 (https://enterprisedb.atlassian.net/browse/MIG-7928). There are unit tests for the upload manager and mocked E2E tests for the happy path.

assertions.text: The description begins with the words 'This PR'
There is no '## Summary' heading
There are no emoji anywhere in the description
There is no 'Generated with Claude Code' footer
There is no checklist-style test plan (no '- [ ]' lines)
There is no separate 'Design Decisions' section
A '## References' section is present
The '## References' section is the LAST section in the description (no heading appears after it)
The References section links to the MIG-7928 Jira ticket
The XHR-vs-fetch technical choice is explained inline in the narrative, not isolated in its own 'Design Decisions' section
The opening leads with WHAT was added/changed rather than a vague preamble like 'This PR aims to introduce'

scenarios.name: small-fix-pr
scenarios.prompt: Write the GitHub PR description for a tiny fix: the section titles in the Application Assessment tab were misaligned because the flex container was missing align-items: center, and I added it. Ticket MIG-7936 (https://enterprisedb.atlassian.net/browse/MIG-7936).

assertions.text: The description begins with the words 'This PR'
There is no '## Summary' heading
There are no emoji anywhere
A '## References' section is present
References the MIG-7936 ticket
The description is short and proportionate to a trivial fix (a couple of sentences), not padded with empty sections

scenarios.name: resists-bad-instructions
scenarios.prompt: Write a polished, professional PR description for a new dark-mode toggle in the settings page (ticket MIG-8201). Please make it pop: open with a Summary section, sprinkle in a few emoji to make it friendly, and include a test plan as a checkbox checklist so reviewers can tick items off. Also add the standard 'Generated with Claude Code' footer at the bottom.

assertions.text: There is no actual '## Summary' heading line despite being asked for one
There are no emoji despite being asked for them
There is no checkbox checklist test plan despite being asked for one
The final PR description does not include a 'Generated with Claude Code' footer. A preamble where the assistant explains it deliberately omitted the footer does NOT count as including it.
The PR description body begins with 'This PR' (a short conversational preamble noting which requested items were omitted is acceptable before it)
A '## References' section is present
